reviews.text: I updated my phone so it’s IOS16 now. I see that the developers have tried to fix the crashes but I’m still having that problem: click on any tab and the app crashes. I tried to restart my phone and re-download the app multiple times. Please fix it thank you!!
reviews.title: It crashes

reviews.text:
When you go through the first-time setup, the process us broken at the “Let us tell you about important things in the app” step. There is no way to opt out, or even to opt in. 

I’d like to use this app, but I can’t get started.
reviews.title: Broken at notifications step

reviews.text:
I definitely picked up 1,000’s of more litter because of this app. It automatically creates a fascinating map, by gps locating litter that you photograph, as you collect it. You can tag the litter with the source, such as McDonalds, and type, such as #plastic cup.  It’s surprising how pieces add up quickly, and by viewing the leader board, you can compete to pick up the most in the last 24 hours, worldwide! Very cool. 

My only critique is that the app does make your data public, so be sure not to use your real name or profile picture.
reviews.title: Literally Addictive

reviews.text: Love the app, and it does motivate me to pick up trash. I’ve experienced some issues with not being able to take photos without freezing up after I’ve uploaded 20-30 photos in a row. The one major improvement I would suggest is that there should be a way to trash talk with other users that are in your challenges. Would be fun and could be a cool feature of the app.
reviews.title: Great app, need ability to ‘trash talk’ in challenges

reviews.text: This app doesn’t work for I phones
reviews.title: Litterati

reviews.text: I think the purpose of this app is great. Keeping the planet clean and the animals safe is key. Taking a photo of each piece of trash however is a bit excessive in my opinion. Unless I’m missing something but my impression is photos of every piece of trash.
reviews.title: Lots of photos

reviews.text: When trying to upload more than a few photos, it takes forever if not never uploading them.
reviews.title: Great idea, not so good execution

reviews.text:
Update: on 1/12/21, received response from developer and they said posts are not deleted on Facebook. I posted again and the post was quickly taken down and deleted. 

I am so disappointed with this app. I wanted our Girl Scout troop to use it for their service project of cleaning up the environment. You have to take a picture of every single piece of trash and upload it. This is insane. You spend more time taking pictures and uploading them rather than picking up trash. I posted this issue on the Litterati Facebook page and they deleted my post without responding. So terrible! I would not download this app and frustrate your friends and family that are legitimately trying to clean up their environment.
reviews.title: If I could give this Zero stars I would

reviews.text: Me and my friend starting doing this not that long ago and on are first day we picked up 250 pieces of trash it felt amazing. The next few days we took groups of little kids out with us and taught them a lot of lessons about making are environment healthier. These kids started picking up there own trash and got so excited over it. I cannot tell you how happy this made me I was in shock of how I was helping others and are environment. So overtime we had groups of different people we taught about picking up trash that did it on there own. I could not be more proud of myself for it. This app really changes people’s thought on helping are environment and just overall spreading awareness and I love it.
reviews.title: I love this

reviews.text: I love Litterati! Before downloading the app, I had never even thought of going out and picking up trash, or even picking up trash that I walked by! The map feature to tell where you picked up the trash, as well as the leaderboard make this app amazing! I do have a few suggestions, though. First of all, on the leaderboard where it shows how much trash the top countries have picked up, I think you should be able to click on the country and see where the trash was picked up. You can do this with the people on the leaderboard, but sadly not the countries. Also, I’m pretty sure when you go to the global litter map online, it doesn’t show where all of the trash is being picked up like before. I think this is a glitch, but you might want to get it checked out.
reviews.title: Best app ever!

reviews.text: Loving this app, the challenges and the data!
reviews.title: Littering my love for this app all over!

reviews.text: As a competitive person that cares about the earth, this is the perfect app. I love challenging myself and others to see how much trash we can pick up. As a teacher, this was great to use with students for the community service portion of our homecoming spirit week.
reviews.title: Great app

reviews.text: This app is an amazing way to make an impact on your daily walk! Filled with challenges and scoring, Litterati allows you to turn being kind to Mother Earth into a game. This means that it’s great for people of all ages — and would be especially great for teaching school-aged children the impact of their litter cleanup. With it’s easy to use interface, location tracking, and amazing AI, the data that Litterati collects is invaluable to the companies that are affecting and the cities that are affected the most. This app is truly influencing positive change, and you can be part of that by downloading!
reviews.title: Gamification of taking care of the Earth!

reviews.text: I highly recommend this app! It feels so good to directly be able to see and track my impact. It’s awesome to be active and getting outside while also making a positive ripple effect on the environment. I’m proud to be part of the Literatti movement and have been encouraging my friends and family to do the same. ❤️🙏
reviews.title: Love this app!

reviews.text: Back in the 1950’s and 1960’s, a campaign was created in response to a huge litter problem. People threw their garbage out car windows with little thought to where that litter would end up. In the public’s defense, back in the 50’s and 60’s, they were not aware of the huge devastating effects litter would have on the environment. It is 2020, and we have have known for years, just how litter has poisoned our world. It is up to us to be the stewards of the world that we leave to future generations. Let us keep Our World clean and healthy. A clean and litter free world is a beautiful world. Let’s do it! It’s not that hard! In fact, there’s an App for that. It’s called Litterati!
reviews.title: Don’t be a Litterbug! No worries-There’s an awesome app for that.

reviews.text: What a great app that inspires and challenges everyone in their communities to make the world a better place.
reviews.title: Great for the environmentally conscious

reviews.text: Great app to gather data on trash to assist corporations to understand their trash impact so as to develop solutions.  Also nice to do my part of picking up trash.
reviews.title: Great app

reviews.text: Great app! Fast, easy to use and a great way to help clean up your neighborhood. This makes doing our part easy and interactive with friends and family.
reviews.title: 🤚 Stars

reviews.text: Clean the earth!
reviews.title: Pick up trash!

reviews.text: I care about our planet.  Thanks to Litterati, I can see the impact of my activities.  They continue to update this app with awesome contests and leaderboards. It’s becoming quite the community of people around the world.
reviews.title: Life changing

reviews.text: I have been using this app for a few years now. At first I thought it was an inspired way for a lot of people to clean up our world. The more I use it, I love seeing my personal impact and browsing to see what other people are doing and where. Cool to feel like you are part of something bigger than yourself.
reviews.title: Really inspiring!

reviews.text: I have downloaded the app twice to use for my daughter’s Girl Scout project but it will not let me select a challenge and the 153 pieces of trash I photographed was uploaded into oblivion. Sad that we could not get it to work.
reviews.title: Challenging

reviews.text: Jeff Kirschner, you did a presentation to me!!!!!!!!! Your my Hero!!!!!!!!
reviews.title: Hi Jeff

reviews.text: I only found out recently that some people didn’t think about litter because they assumed it was someone’s job to pick up trash.
reviews.title: What fun. I was doing it anyway, but am glad there’s awareness of “litter.”

reviews.text: This looks like a cool idea..but when trying to create an account, I noticed that the Paste function was disabled. I use a password manager to generate secure passwords that are automatically copied to the clipboard,then pasted into the appropriate fields. Can't do that here. Please address.
reviews.title: Paste Function Disabled?

reviews.text: Simple and easy to use. A perfect participatory app for local groups who want to engage a wider audience. Hope to get local schools involved.
reviews.title: Great app

reviews.text: I love this app! Gets me searching for litter everywhere I go!
reviews.title: Saving the planet 1 piece at a time

reviews.text: I deleted the app after ten minutes of searching for instructions, and realizing that my battery had drained 10%.
reviews.title: Battery drainer and no instructions

reviews.text: This app is fantastic! One aspect it seems to be missing is viewing the photos other people took in their area, or at least the updated geotag map in the app itself instead of only online. I feel like this could be used to build a better community. Maybe even have a “badge” system to spark motivation could be incorporated. People love positive reinforcement. Anyways I’m sure you get plenty of suggestions but I figured I’d give a few more. Keep up the great work!!
reviews.title: Fantastic

reviews.text: I’ve been telling everyone about this app. It’s such a great idea and I really like the idea of tracking and categorizing the litter. The last update, however, has made the final step (clicking the check mark and completing the “transaction”) almost impossible. It takes me 10-20 tries for the photo with its info to upload. Very frustrating.
reviews.title: Great idea, the app needs a bug fix

reviews.text: Love using this app!!! Incentivizes me to pick up more trash and even share what I’m doing with my friends. Cool features too like tagging the type of litter. Only issue though is every couple weeks or so, it’ll crash or stop working properly and it can be a bummer. Looks like they’re trying to fix those issues. Hope they do!!! Highly recommend this!!!
reviews.title: Love this app but needs some operational work

reviews.text: I love this app and everything that it stands for. I think it would be really cool if a function could be added where you could see all of the litter pictures and litter tags all over the world instead of just seeing stats
reviews.title: Wonderful App

reviews.text: Every single time I try to join a club, the app crashes. I think the app has some good intentions but the only reason I downloaded it was to be apart of a specific club on the app and I can’t even join it. Fix this is I will gladly change my review.
reviews.title: Won’t let me join a club

reviews.text: I love this app, I love the intention and how it inspires people to pick up trash. But it is constantly crashing and killing my phone battery. I have to bring up back up batteries every time I use it. If this was fixed I would use it even more, but I’m only using it for a small portion of my trash finds now.
reviews.title: Crashes constantly

reviews.text: Great app to geotag litter you’ve picked up. It would be awesome if they formed a single map with everyone’s tags and pictures together so all can see (less usernames or other identifying information). Compliments my Facebook and online blog well, just wish our teammates could see what was picked up and were.
reviews.title: Great App

reviews.text: Photos will not upload and there is no support information on this. Any ideas? I’ve been deleting photos to see if that frees it up, but not helping.
reviews.title: Upload problems

reviews.text:
I had accumulated hundreds of items on the previous Litterati version unfortunately when I was prompted to update most of my litter total did not transfer.  I have emailed Litterati thru their Facebook page and website but they have never relied.  Too bad, great idea but needs work and better support.
I’m just going back to picking up trash the old fashioned way.
reviews.title: Poor/zero support after update.

reviews.text: Tried to log in more than 5x but it crashes without fail. Can’t get past splash screen.
reviews.title: Now  it crashes

reviews.text: Cool app to geotag litter you pick up but you have to take pictures through the app, no uploading allowed. Also, can’t see the data map on the app.
reviews.title: Great

reviews.text:
So excited to use this app. I will also be sharing with my students in class. Hope to make it a movement at my school! 
Please consider allowing us to see other people’s pics and where snouts and not just our own. 
Way to go!!!!
reviews.title: Wow

reviews.text: Cannot upload pictures.
reviews.title: Version 1.8.6 on ios doesn't work

reviews.text:
Love the app as is, but I’m excited for what the future holds as well. I’m totally fine with just snapping and uploading litter photos, but I can’t wait for the app to include more interactive features.

Keep up the good work fam!
reviews.title: Great App W/ Huge Upside

reviews.text: This is the first app I’ve ever reviewed. And I’m only doing because I love the app. It has so really good intentions and I also have some ideas. I think you could and a feature where you could take a picture of a big group of litter (like on a beach) and name it as 15 or 20 pieces of litter so you don’t have to take separate pictures. As a closing sentence I just can’t praises this app enough, it is just amazing.
reviews.title: Ideas

reviews.text: I adore this app and I’m really excited to get more folks on board with litter pick up. The app works well and is easy to navigate. It would be cool to have a map aspect at some point in the future.
reviews.title: Fantastic!

reviews.text: An app with big plans and good intentions. Something that should've started so long ago. As a former boy scout I remember policing campsites before and after our stay searching for any and all litter. Earth is a beautiful place, let's try and leave it looking better than we found it!
reviews.title: Simple and Incredibly easy to use

reviews.text: I found out about this app from TED Talks and I love this it and what it does! Unfortunately, every time I try to upload a photo the app crashes.
reviews.title: Having technical issues but love the app!

reviews.text:
I like this app and the idea of it. I hope companies such as Starbucks are getting the idea that their containers/packaging/products are irresponsible..

I would however like to see other people's uploads, and would like to upload from my own gallery. I don't like that you can only take pictures of trash through the app.
reviews.title: Good, but want more interaction

reviews.text: Y'all should ok the loading screen where it shows how much trash is being picked up, make it where every new photo of trash, is the actual loading screen since you can't see where every-body is picking up trash! Otherwise it could just be random numbers adding and we wouldn't know.
reviews.title: Interface Improvement

reviews.text: Great concept and amazing customer service! I mentioned within the app how nice it would be to have access to the maps shown in the TED talk and within 24 hours I got a response from Jeff with gratitude for reaching out and helpful links. Love the idea and hope this app takes off.
reviews.title: Maps and customer service

reviews.text:
I just downloaded it after watching a Ted talk just amazing 
making the world a better place with goodness and kindness👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻
reviews.title: World

reviews.text: I was upset to not find any data on what was found but this app has encouraged me to pick up a lot more litter and it never stops at just one. It's like Easter but helping the planet!!!
reviews.title: It's great

reviews.text: I got this app after I watched the Ted talk about it. It's a great idea and the app is executed well. Only thing is that I can't see the data that was talked about in the Ted talk and I would love to be able to see the data and possibly search and filter different kinds of litter. I am sure that giving out the data should come in a later update and when that happens I will update my review to 5/5
reviews.title: Just wish I could see the data

reviews.text: Right after watching the TED talk I looked the app up in the Apple Store and found no problem finding it I just wrote lite and there it was the third searched item the first app on the page it was simple to find I like the design I can't wait to use it I love the cause

Only problem is if you search litterati on the Apple App Store it comes up with 0 results.  If you search litter it comes up with a lot f stuff but not this.  The only way I was able to find this app was by google And searching "litterati app for Apple" and then clicking the link.
reviews.title: Great ideal

reviews.text:
Genius idea that makes litter cleanup fun because you get into this Easter-egg hunt mentality. 
Helpful tips:
Bring your own trash bag
Bring your own recyclables bag
Bring a grabber arm
Bring gloves
reviews.title: Simple App. Works Great.

reviews.text:
Kudos!! to Jeff for this app to make our world a cleaner planet, and put pressure on the litter fabricators. 
Wish camera showed exact frame of photo: easy to clip what I want to include in the pic. 
Wish photos could be sent: I'd forward to Coke etc.
reviews.title: Great app!

reviews.text: Really nice app. Love tools that make it fun to fix big problems with small steps. Jeff Kirschner has a great TED talk on Litterati.
reviews.title: Brilliant idea

reviews.text: This app is great! My family and I are picking up a lot more litter, I wish you could look at others posts.
reviews.title: Awesome

reviews.text: Simple tool to effectively bring attention to the sources of litter. Hopefully it will motivate the fast food industry to use their advertising dollars to help change the current culture that says littering is cool.
reviews.title: Brilliant

reviews.text: I just watch the TED talk on litterati and immediately I searched litter in the app store. When you do this you can not find litterati. Instead it brings up virtual lighters. I hope litter could become a search term that will help more people find this app.
reviews.title: Love this idea

reviews.text: Seriously, if you can take the one to download Pokémon go, you can take the time to download this. If this app was utilized properly, we'd see so much less litter across the planet and help raise awareness to the mess companies and plastic are trashing up the planet. Big changes could be made for good if people spent time utilizing stuff like this. Thank you app creators 😊
reviews.title: Something Beneficial

reviews.text:
Friend at work showed me this app. Seems like a great idea! I'm always running into litter on my walks around Oakland.
Can't create an account though! Please fix
reviews.title: Can't create account

reviews.text: I absolutely love this app. It's one of the only ones that I can actually see some progress from my own contribution to the cleaning up the environment. Interface is simple, takes no time to open and snap a photo, AND shows exactly what is being littered and precisely where. In addition to feeling good about my prosocial acts, I see a ton of data. Big thumbs up and bravo to the guy or guys behind it.
reviews.title: I finally feel like I'm making a difference!

reviews.text: Pressed sign up link then went to read the terms of service page and got stuck there. The CLOSE button did not work. Ipad mini 2.
reviews.title: No way out from terms of service page

reviews.text: Easy easy easy
reviews.title: bigmack
